Block.Para: First Session, Thirty-seventh Parliament,
49-50 Elizabeth II, 2001
SENATE OF CANADA
BILL S-13
An Act respecting the declaration of royal assent by the Governor General in the Queen芒聙聶s name to bills passed by the Houses of Parliament
first reading, February 7, 2001

Available on the Parliament of Canada Web Site at the following address:
http://www.parl.gc.ca
This enactment provides an alternative procedure for royal assent to a bill.
1st Session, 37th Parliament,
49-50 Elizabeth II, 2001
senate of canada
BILL S-13
An Act respecting the declaration of royal assent by the Governor General in the Queen芒聙聶s name to bills passed by the Houses of Parliament
Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:
1. This Act may be cited as the Royal Assent Act.
2. Royal assent by the Governor General in the Queen芒聙聶s name to a bill passed by the Houses of Parliament may be declared, during the session in which both Houses passed the bill,
(a) in the form and manner customary before the coming into force of this Act; or
(b) by a written declaration, unless the bill is the first appropriation bill presented for assent in a session.

Block.MarginalNote: When customary form and manner to be used
Block.Para: 3. A declaration of royal assent in the form and manner referred to in paragraph 2(a) must occur on at least one occasion in each calendar year.
4. A written declaration of royal assent shall be reported in each House of Parliament by the Speaker of that House or by the person acting as the Speaker.

Block.MarginalNote: Date of assent by written declaration
Block.Para: 5. Where royal assent is declared by a written declaration, the date of assent is the day on which the declaration is reported in both Houses of Parliament or, if it is reported in each House on different days, the later of those days.
6. A written declaration of royal assent is not a statutory instrument within the meaning of the Statutory Instruments Act.
7. No royal assent is invalid by reason only that section 3 is not complied with.
Published under authority of the Senate of Canada
脗聞
